one_line_takeaway: JailNewsBench shows current LLMs (including GPT-5, Gemini 2.5, and Claude 4) are readily jailbroken into producing harmful fake news (up to 86.3% ASR), with markedly weaker defenses for non-English/non-U.S. content and for fake news generally compared to toxicity or social-bias content, despite models internally encoding truth/falsity signals they fail to surface.
key_finding: Across the 9 models, maximum ASR under jailbreak reached 86.3% (Qwen3-4B) and maximum average harmfulness score was 3.5/5 (harmfulness metrics use a 0-4 scale, so figures in Table 2 range up to ~3.5). Even safety-aligned frontier models had high jailbreak ASR: GPT-5 75.3%, Gemini 2.5 77.6%, Claude 4 76.1% (Claude 4 had the lowest ASR and lowest average harmfulness score among all 9 models). Harmfulness scores rose in the order Explicit < Original < Jailbreak, while ASR showed the opposite ordering, indicating a refusal-avoidance/harmfulness trade-off. Defense was strongest for U.S./English-region news and weakest for non-English/non-U.S. regions (Figure 1), and simply translating non-English news/instructions into English did not significantly improve defense scores (Wilcoxon p<0.05 test found no significant difference for the sub-metric scores despite disfluency-rate improvements for typologically distant languages like Korean, Chinese, Japanese). Internal linear probes detected self-generated fake news far better than external (surface-level) prompting for all white-box models (e.g., DeepSeek-70B: 82.6% internal vs 62.0% external F1; Llama3-8B: 66.0% internal vs 56.3% external F1), a statistically significant gap (McNemar p<0.01) for every model, suggesting models internally 'know' content is false even when they generate and fail to flag it externally. ASR was consistently higher for fake-news rewrites than for toxicity or social-bias rewrites of the same seed instructions across all 9 models (Table 5, deltas ranging roughly -1.9 to -5.2 points), showing fake news is comparatively under-defended. Across 7 existing safety/jailbreak datasets totaling 417,787 instances, only 1,397 (0.33%) were fake-news-related versus 17,558 (4.20%) toxic and 16,159 (3.87%) social-bias-related -- roughly an order of magnitude less coverage for fake news. The paper's own sub-metrics-based LLM-as-a-Judge achieved Spearman rank correlation of 0.68 with human rankings, significantly higher than a single-score OneScore ablation (0.52) and three prior fake-news-detector baselines (0.41, 0.36, 0.39). The paper's jailbreak prompt produced significantly higher ASR and harmfulness scores than three prior fake-news-generation prompt families (HG/AMG/CMG) for all 9 models (Table 8, all differences significant at p<0.01).
core_contribution: The paper introduces JailNewsBench, the first multi-lingual and multi-regional benchmark for evaluating LLM robustness to jailbreak-induced fake news generation. It covers 34 regions and 22 languages with ~300k seed instructions built from real news articles (sampled from the babel-briefings multi-lingual news dataset via NewsAPI), each rewritten via a template that embeds one of four malicious motivations (Financial, Political, Social, Psychological, taxonomy from Wardle & Derakhshan 2017). The benchmark defines two baseline prompt settings (Original, Explicit) plus five jailbreak attack methods (Role Play, System Override, Research Front, Negative Prompting, Context Overload) applicable to black-box models. It also proposes an 8-sub-metric LLM-as-a-Judge harmfulness evaluator (Faithfulness, Verifiability, Adherence, Scope, Scale, Formality, Subjectivity, Agitativeness, scored 0-4, judged by the average of GPT-5/Gemini 2.5/Claude 4), validated against human relative-ranking meta-evaluation (83% inter-annotator agreement on a 32-instance sample). Using this benchmark the authors evaluate 9 LLMs, analyze regional/language disparities in defense, test whether translating non-English content to English improves defenses, probe internal vs. external self-detection of self-generated fake news, and audit coverage of 'fake news' as a category in seven existing safety datasets.
threat_model: Attacker is a malicious user with black-box or white-box API access to an LLM who wants the model to generate a fake news article (intentionally false, misleading news) targeting a specific region/language, motivated by financial, political, social, or psychological gain. The attacker uses one of five prompt-level jailbreak techniques (role-play framing, system-prompt override instructions, framing the request as research, negative/prohibitive phrasing that induces the model to reveal the content while 'refusing', or context-overload via 512 tokens of irrelevant preceding text) to bypass the model's refusal behavior. The paper explicitly does not address fake news that arises unintentionally from model hallucination/error -- only intentional, jailbreak-induced generation.
limitations: Regional coverage is skewed toward developed/politically stable countries because of the paper's own ethics-driven exclusion criteria (special fake-news legislation, Fragile States Index threshold, Conflict Watchlist 2025, recency cutoff), so the benchmark does not represent the global distribution of languages/regions where fake news risk may be highest. The temporal buffer (articles from Aug 2020-Nov 2021) may bias robustness estimates optimistically if models have since learned to recognize these events as factual. The dataset and generated fake-news examples are not publicly disclosed in the paper (dual-use non-disclosure) and release is described as 'restricted-access,' limiting external reproducibility of the raw harmful content. Seed instruction generation itself relies on GPT-5 to select motivations and produce instructions, and the harmfulness judge is an ensemble of GPT-5/Gemini 2.5/Claude 4 -- i.e., the paper's grounding and evaluation both depend heavily on the same class of frontier commercial models being assessed, introducing potential judge-model correlation effects. Internal-probe self-detection experiments are restricted to white-box models only, since black-box APIs do not expose hidden states.

datasets_used: babel-briefings (Leeb & Scholkopf 2024) via NewsAPI, source of factual news articles
JailNewsBench (this paper's own dataset): ~300k seed instructions, 34 regions, 22 languages, 80/10/10 train/dev/test split
quantitative_results: Max ASR across 9 models under jailbreak = 86.3% (Qwen3-4B)
Max average harmfulness score = 3.5 (0-4 sub-metric scale)
Jailbreak ASR: GPT-5 75.3%, Gemini 2.5 77.6%, Claude 4 76.1%, DeepSeek-70B 82.0%, DeepSeek-8B 84.9%, Qwen3-30B 79.4%, Qwen3-4B 86.3%, Llama3-70B 78.0%, Llama3-8B 85.2%
Claude 4 lowest ASR and lowest average harmfulness score among all 9 models tested
External self-detection F1: GPT-5 68.2 (best), Llama3-8B 56.3 (worst); chance rate 50.0

Internal linear-probe self-detection F1: DeepSeek-70B 82.6, DeepSeek-8B 77.3, Qwen3-30B 73.4, Qwen3-4B 70.7, Llama3-70B 68.5, Llama3-8B 66.0 -- all significantly higher than external detection (McNemar p<0.01)
Fake-news coverage across 7 existing safety datasets: 1,397/417,787 (0.33%) vs toxic 17,558 (4.20%) vs social bias 16,159 (3.87%)

Meta-evaluation Spearman correlation with human rankings: Sub-metrics (this paper's method) 0.68, OneScore 0.52, Chen & Shu 2023 baseline 0.41, Hu et al. 2024 baseline 0.36, Alghamdi et al. 2024 baseline 0.39
Inter-annotator agreement on human meta-evaluation (32 sampled instances) = 83%

Own jailbreak prompt vs. HG/AMG/CMG prior prompts: consistently higher ASR and harmfulness for all 9 models, significant at p<0.01 (e.g., GPT-5 ASR deltas -32.4/-33.5/-35.0 points relative to HG/AMG/CMG, i.e. prior prompts underperform by that much)
baselines_compared: Fake-news detector baselines for LLM-as-a-Judge meta-evaluation: Chen & Shu (2023), Hu et al. (2024), Alghamdi et al. (2024)
OneScore (single holistic-score LLM-as-a-Judge ablation, no sub-metrics)
Prior fake-news-generation prompts: Hallucination Generation (HG), Arbitrary Misinformation Generation (AMG), Controllable Misinformation Generation (CMG), all from Chen & Shu (2024)

Baseline prompt settings within JailNewsBench: Original (no jailbreak) and Explicit (explicit fake-news request, no jailbreak) vs. the 5 jailbreak attacks (Role Play, System Override, Research Front, Negative Prompting, Context Overload)
9 target LLMs evaluated as the systems under test: GPT-5, Gemini 2.5, Claude 4, DeepSeek-70B, DeepSeek-8B, Qwen3-30B, Qwen3-4B, Llama3-70B, Llama3-8B
